titleStmt.title: SII 18.80: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 80. (B.K. No. 145 of 1933-34.) BIJAPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: This inscription referring itself to the reign of Bhuvanaikamalladēva is dated in Śaka 996, Ānanda, Pushya s11u. 5, Thursday, Uttarāyāṇa-saṁkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1074, December 25, Thursday. The Samkrānti however fell on the previous day.

It records a grant of land to god Svayambhu-Siddēs11varadēva of Vijayāpura, by daṇḍanāyaka Nakimayya who requested Ballavarasa who was camping at Vaṅkāpura to have a temple constructed for the god. The gift is stated to have been made for feeding and clothing the students of Yōgēśvara-paṇḍita and the other teachers. Nākimayya was governing Tardavāḍi One-thousand at the time of the gift. The record gives an eulogistic account of the āchārya of the temple Yōgēs11vara-paṇḍita, in Sanskrit verse. It is published in Indian Antiquary, Vol. X, p. 126).

In continuation of this, and in the same characters, is engraved another record referring to Tribhuvanamalla (apparently Vikrāmāditya VI). So it is likely that the grant mentioned above was put on the stone at a later date though actually made on the date cited.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: Digital edition of SII 18.80 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
